headline: Kirkwood Pulse • March 06, 2026
overview:
project:
  developer: Double Eagle Development
  site: Former public works site, Kirkwood
  proposal_date: January 8th
  proposal_details:
  homes: 203
  buildings: 6
  parking_spots: 303
  building_type: all-residential
  architecture_style: traditional (pitched roofs, chimneys, covered porches)
  unique_features:
  -: new through-street connecting Taylor and Fillmore
  -: townhome style apartments with ground-floor private entrances
  -: Breezestak layout offering varied floor plans
  apartment_mix:
  1_bedroom: similar to recent developments
  2_bedroom: similar to recent developments
  3_bedroom: similar to recent developments
council_decision_factors:
  preferred_response: Double Eagle’s proposal
  likely_most_lucrative: True
  market_value_considerations: All-residential with more homes yields higher value
  architecture_preference: Conservative, traditional style favored over modern
  reference_projects: Bemiston Place Apartments in Clayton
  walk-up apartment layouts: Supported by Council
criticisms_and_regrets:
  trail_integration: Lack of value placed on Grant’s Trail extension and related retail potential
  phased_development: Suggested phased approach for better long-term community return
  walkability: Project considered too auto-oriented with excessive asphalt and curb cuts
  parking: Preference for structured parking to improve community space and walkability
  building_code: Recommendation for Council to add flexibility to building codes for walk-up designs
additional_notes:
  KPAC_parking_lot: Not included in current development, leaving future development options open
  construction_timeline: Grant’s Trail construction to start in about two years overview: Kirkwood City Council, dominated by a 6-1 Republican majority, has taken a sharply partisan and conservative turn, departing from previous norms of cautious, community-focused governance.
key_events:
  -:
  event: Abrupt dismissal of city administrator Russ Hawes after more than a decade, with no formal transition or successor named.
  -:
  event: Conservative activists successfully installed six of seven council members in the April 2, 2024 election, pushing a unified slate aimed at removing Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion initiatives from the school district.
political_context:
  partisan_shift: Despite nonpartisan elections, council members have strong ties to the national Conservative Movement and Trump-era Republican politics.
  donation_analysis:
  total_republican_donations: 30225
  total_democratic_donations: 710
  notable_recipients:
  -: Jason Smith (Republican House Ways & Means Chair)
  -: Senator Eric Schmitt (MAGA-aligned)
  -: Georgia Republicans Kelly Loeffler and David Perdue
  -: Donald J. Trump for President, Inc.
  spousal_donations: Also predominantly Republican-aligned, reinforcing the council's partisan commitments.
governance_style:
  description: Council appears focused on political consolidation, punishing Democratic opponents, and rewarding Republican allies rather than community-focused leadership.
  examples:
  -: Dismissal of infrastructure cost-saving ideas due to partisan bias.
  -: Pursuit of selling the city’s water utility, potentially raising costs despite short-term revenue gains.
implications: The council's approach risks increasing costs for residents and prioritizes political agendas over long-term community welfare. new_interim_city_manager:

  resignation_effective_date: 2026-04-03
  consulting_period: 60 days following resignation

  current_work_status: Full-time until early April, then consulting

city_council_notes:

  comments:
  -: Appreciates Scott Davis and looks forward to working with him during transition
  -: No search for permanent city manager planned until after new council members take office
  -: Two new council members will participate fully in hiring process
  search_firm_engagement: No plans to engage outside search firm at this time

items.title: How Double Eagle Won Over the Council
items.excerpt: On January 8th, Kirkwood City Council will hold a public hearing on Double Eagle Development’s proposal for the former public works site. The proposal, the Council’s preferred response of the six they received, calls for 203 homes across six all-residential buildings and 303 surface parking spots. In reviewing each of the RFP responses, a picture of the Council’s decision-making process

items.title: Kirkwood’s 6-1 Republican Majority
items.excerpt: In a Friday evening news dump the week before Thanksgiving, Kirkwood City Council announced that they would be parting ways with city administrator Russ Hawes. There would be no formal transition to a named successor, no transfer of institutional knowledge, and no grand send-off. Instead, the end for the man who had managed the city’s day-to-day operations for more than
items.ai_summary:
overview: Kirkwood City Council, dominated by a 6-1 Republican majority, has taken a sharply partisan and conservative turn, departing from previous norms of cautious, community-focused governance.
key_events:
  -:
  event: Abrupt dismissal of city administrator Russ Hawes after more than a decade, with no formal transition or successor named.
  -:
  event: Conservative activists successfully installed six of seven council members in the April 2, 2024 election, pushing a unified slate aimed at removing Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion initiatives from the school district.
political_context:
  partisan_shift: Despite nonpartisan elections, council members have strong ties to the national Conservative Movement and Trump-era Republican politics.
  donation_analysis:
  total_republican_donations: 30225
  total_democratic_donations: 710
  notable_recipients:
  -: Jason Smith (Republican House Ways & Means Chair)
  -: Senator Eric Schmitt (MAGA-aligned)
  -: Georgia Republicans Kelly Loeffler and David Perdue
  -: Donald J. Trump for President, Inc.
  spousal_donations: Also predominantly Republican-aligned, reinforcing the council's partisan commitments.
governance_style:
  description: Council appears focused on political consolidation, punishing Democratic opponents, and rewarding Republican allies rather than community-focused leadership.
  examples:
  -: Dismissal of infrastructure cost-saving ideas due to partisan bias.
  -: Pursuit of selling the city’s water utility, potentially raising costs despite short-term revenue gains.
implications: The council's approach risks increasing costs for residents and prioritizes political agendas over long-term community welfare.

items.title: Scott Davis Named Interim City Manager
items.excerpt: Webster Groves has named veteran city leader Scott Davis to serve as interim city manager. City Manager Marie Peoples last month announced her resignation from the post, effective April 3, plus a 60-day consulting period with the city to follow. Davis, who has served as the city’s deputy city manager of strategic innovation since being promoted to the position that

items.title: “Wet/Wild” Watercolors Show
items.excerpt: The Old Orchard Gallery, 39 S. Old Orchard Ave., will present an art exhibit featuring the watercolors of about 20 painters from the weekly “Wet/Wild/Watercolor Wednesday” painting sessions throughout the year. The show runs March 7 to May 4, with…

items.title: Man Charged With Sexually Abusing Child At KW Walmart
items.excerpt: A St. Louis man has been charged with sex crimes after approaching and sexually abusing a 7-year-old child at the Kirkwood Walmart on March 1. Authorities say the man also approached three other children in the store that day, and believe there could be additional victims. Esmin Pasic, 33, of the 3200 block of Morganford Road in St. Louis, was
items.ai_summary:
incident_date: 2026-03-01
location: Kirkwood Walmart, 1202 S. Kirkwood Road
suspect:
  name: Esmin Pasic
  age: 33
  address: 3200 block of Morganford Road, St. Louis
  criminal_history: No prior charges in Missouri
charges:
  -: enticement of a child
  -: sexual abuse of a child
bond: 250000
details: Pasic approached and sexually abused a 7-year-old child inside the Kirkwood Walmart by grabbing and stroking the child's genitals and rubbing his own genitals against the child's shoulder. The child identified Pasic in a police lineup. Surveillance footage confirmed the incident and showed Pasic was in the store for hours. Authorities are aware of at least three other children approached by Pasic that day and believe there may be additional victims. Pasic also visited a nearby Target store after Walmart.
law_enforcement_advice: Parents who were at the Walmart or Target stores on March 1 with children are urged to discuss the outing with their kids and contact the Kirkwood Police Department at 314-822-5858 if they have concerns.
arrest_info: Pasic was arrested at his home on March 2 and is currently held on $250,000 bond.
